title: MATERNITY HOUSING AND SUPPORT - 211info
text: ROAD TO HOPE MATERNITY HOUSING AND SUPPORT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides stable, secure, and nurturing residential care in a home environment for pregnant women and women with children. Residents may stay at any point through their pregnancy and stay up to approximately one year after the birth of their baby. Residents have access to additional support with gaining OB/GYN care, high school/GED education completion, gaining employment, budgeting, individual counseling, advocacy and case management, developing parenting skills, and support working towards family reunification. Participation in faith-based worship service of choosing is required. Accepts mothers with one additional child, when capacity allows. Does not accept couples or individuals with pets of any kind. Confidential Address Eligibility: Pregnant women age 18 or older who are in crisis (homeless, fleeing domestic violence, etc.). Women can be pregnant and have an additional child under the age of 3 depending on program capacity. Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (503) 648-4227 Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (503) 648-4227 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for appointment or complete form on website. A full interview with program staff is required for residential services. FEES: Main maternity home is free. Transitional and graduate housing has small monthly program fee. DOCUMENTS: Photo ID and proof of pregnancy preferred (can assist with documentation if needed) LANGUAGES: English, interpretation by arrangement AREA SERVED: State of Oregon Services: Adoption Services Case/Care Management Intensive Family Reunification Services Life Skills Education Maternity Homes If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. ROAD TO HOPE
